task.objective: Add a tabbed interface to the compare page that allows switching between "Branch Mode" (select two branches for automatic latest-run comparison) and "Execution Mode" (existing behavior of selecting individual executions). Branch Mode is the primary workflow and should be the default.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - Compare Page Enhancement
context.background:
The current compare page only supports "Execution Mode" where users must manually select individual executions. The most common use case is "compare my feature branch against main" which requires finding specific executions.
The API already supports branch-based comparison via `baseline_branch` and `current_branch` parameters (see app/api/compare/route.ts lines 44-64), but this capability is not exposed in the UI.
Branch Mode should: 1. Let users select two branches 2. Optionally filter by suite 3. Automatically use the latest execution from each branch 4. Show which executions were selected for transparency
IMPORTANT: The existing URL params (?baseline=123&current=456) must continue to work for backwards compatibility and shareable links.
context.audience: - Developers comparing feature branches against main/master - QA engineers reviewing branch stability before merge
context.impact: VERY HIGH - This is the most valuable improvement, reducing comparison workflow from ~30 seconds to ~5 seconds (2 clicks instead of searching + scrolling)

existing_code.file: export interface BranchStatistics { branch: string last_run: string | null execution_count: number pass_rate: number last_status: "success" | "failure" | "running" | null }

functional.requirement: Add mode toggle tabs: "Branch Mode" and "Execution Mode"
Branch Mode shows BranchSelector components for baseline and current
Branch Mode includes optional suite filter dropdown
Branch Mode calls API with baseline_branch and current_branch params
Show resolved execution details in comparison header
Preserve backwards compatibility with existing ?baseline=&current= URLs
Set Branch Mode as default when no params present
Add proper error handling for branch not found

non_functional.requirement: Preserve existing Execution Mode behavior exactly
Memoize callbacks to prevent unnecessary re-renders

solution.approach: 1. Update page.tsx to pass branchStats (full statistics) to CompareClient 2. Add CompareMode type and mode state (determine from URL params) 3. Add state for branch selection: baselineBranch, currentBranch, selectedSuite 4. Create mode toggle tabs at top of selection section 5. Render BranchSelector when in branch mode, ExecutionSelector when in execution mode 6. Modify fetchComparison to use branch params when in branch mode 7. Update URL params based on mode and selection

steps.step: 2. Verify "Branch Mode" tab is active by default
2. Select a feature branch as current
5. Verify execution info header shows correct branches and commits
2. Verify it opens in Execution Mode with those IDs selected
2. Verify it opens in Branch Mode with those branches selected
1. Select a branch with no executions
2. Verify error message appears: "No execution found for branch: X"
3. Select same branch for both baseline and current
4. Verify error: "Cannot compare a branch with itself"

always.rule: Default to Branch Mode for new users (no URL params)
Update URL params for deep linking and sharing
Show clear feedback about what's being compared
Memoize callbacks to prevent unnecessary re-renders

never.rule: Never break existing Execution Mode functionality
Never break existing URL param support
Never hide the mode toggle (users should always be able to switch)
Never auto-compare without both branches/executions selected

output_format.structure: 1. Modify `app/dashboard/compare/page.tsx` to pass branchStats and initialMode props 2. Modify `app/dashboard/compare/compare-client.tsx` with mode toggle and branch selection
output_format.validation: After implementation: 1. Test both modes work independently 2. Verify URL params persist across page refreshes 3. Verify backwards compatibility with old URLs 4. Verify comparison results match whether using branch or execution mode
